title: Presidential Proclamation on Adjustment of Pharmaceutical and Pharmaceutical Ingredient Imports
summary: A presidential proclamation issued following a report from the Secretary of Commerce investigating the effects of pharmaceutical and pharmaceutical ingredient imports on United States national security. The proclamation addresses findings from an investigation conducted under section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962 and implements adjustments to imports of these products into the United States.

title: Proclamation Adjusting Import Tariffs on Aluminum, Steel, and Copper
summary: This proclamation modifies and strengthens previous executive actions concerning import adjustments for aluminum and steel into the United States, and expands these measures to include copper. The proclamation references and amends Proclamation 9704 from March 8, 2018 regarding aluminum imports, Proclamation 9705 from March 8, 2018 regarding steel imports, and Proclamation 10962 from July 30, 2025. The action consolidates and reinforces trade adjustment policies affecting these three metal commodities through modifications to existing tariff or import restriction frameworks.

title: Executive Order on Citizenship Verification Requirements for Federal Elections
summary: This executive order directs federal agencies to implement procedures for verifying citizenship status in connection with federal elections. The order is issued under presidential authority derived from the Constitution and federal statutes including the Help America Vote Act of 2002 and the National Voter Registration Act of 1993. The order addresses federal requirements related to voter eligibility and election administration processes.

title: Memorandum on Compensation for Transportation Security Administration Personnel During Department of Homeland Security Funding Lapse
summary: This memorandum directs the Secretary of Homeland Security and the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to ensure that Transportation Security Administration officers and employees receive compensation during a lapse in Department of Homeland Security appropriations. The memorandum addresses operational impacts on the air transportation system resulting from the funding gap and establishes procedures for compensating affected TSA personnel to maintain security operations at transportation facilities.
